name: Cartesian Coordinates
description: Describing positions using x, y, and z axes—the most common way to locate things in space.

content.overview: Cartesian coordinates are the most common way to describe where something is. You've probably seen them before—just axes on a graph! {{viz:diagram-01}}
keyPoints: Cartesian = position using (x, y) in 2D or (x, y, z) in 3D
Most intuitive: horizontal and vertical (and depth)
Origin at (0, 0) is where axes cross
Common in maps, graphs, physics problems
content.whyItMatters: You'll use Cartesian coordinates constantly in physics. Every motion problem, every position, every force vector starts here.
